transcript.text: Okay, I have been looking, so I analyzed the kickoff call transcript and clawed and took a look at that.
And then I went through the document that you shared.
So what did, like, from the kickoff call, and let me share my screen.
Okay, so they're launching this new product that may or may not be their pivot.
But right now they're saying it's not a pivot.
So they want to, like, they're targeting, it seems like, a new ICP.
Yeah, yeah.
And they want to do, like, the thought leadership.
And then for this, like, context mix, it says 80% bottom middle of the funnel, 20% top of the funnel.
Was this specifically related to SEO content?
Or does it...
Also factor in, like, the thought leadership.
I think that was in context of all content that they develop.
For this product.
Yep.
Okay.
Prioritize AI optimized over traditional SEO.
my God.
Oh, my God.
It's the same thing.
It's the same thing.
What I...
Okay.
Yeah.
I, like, I was, like, I was going through this transcript.
And I was, like, they're, like, we want to do, like, AEO.
I'm, like, oh, my God.
Where do you think the LLMs get their data from?
They get it from Google.
But never mind.
Okay.
So I think what you have here is good.
I did see that it identified, like, some content gaps.
So, like, open source value prop.
I think...
think it would be worth getting that information from them and then, okay, so you do have like these content pillars here.
I think this is good, so let me just see how many.
Okay, I think this is good.
I just would make sure that we have like a narrative because this is like it's going to be based on just this one interview, right?
So far, yeah.
Okay, so I think like some of the content ideas here, they do overlap.
So instead of.
Like anchoring this in pillars, I would try to like, I could, you could prompt Claude to see like, okay, like an eight week publishing plan.
And like each week you publish, they publish like one blog.
It could be more, it could be like 12 weeks, depending on how much material there is.

But I just think if we, for this type of content, if we try to group it into pillars, like there's going to be overlap really for like, for example, like why your data warehouse can't power AI agents and then what AI agents actually need.
This is probably going to be very similar.
And we might not have enough information to actually write these articles.
So I would recommend like for the thought leadership, like a 10 week, 12 week, whatever, like publishing schedule with like each article for each title we could like.
We bullet points, like, okay, this is what we're going to cover, and you said they're going to publish it on their Substack, right?
Yep.
Yeah, so for their Substack, it doesn't even have to be that long.
I think it could be, like, 700, 800 words.
So we don't need that much information as we would need for, like, a typical blog post.
Like, it could be a lot shorter, but we just want to make sure that for each of these blog posts, we have enough meat to actually, like, write something substantial.

Otherwise, because we did this with hypergrowth, and the articles turned out really well when we had enough information to provide in the blog, but if we didn't, then it would just kind of be running around in circles.
So that's one thing.
I would just, like, frame it around, like, articles rather than pillars.
Okay.
I think I...
It's worth, like, I know Kyle was on the call, so, like, it's worth also, like, checking in with him and seeing what he thinks.
And then.
Yeah, he was on kickoff, but not the interview.
Okay.
All right.
Yeah.
But what is his role on this account?
He's here to help a little bit with strategy and kind of keeping things moving, but he's kind of relying on the Sprint team to be the day-to-day, kind of pushing it forward.
Okay.
All right.
Yeah.
Okay, cool.

I think, yeah, I think definitely I would go, like, move away from the clusters because we do clusters for, like, an ongoing SEO strategy where we can repeatedly, like, replenish the clusters, but for this plan, we just have, like, this finite amount of information.
So, we
could just like do like a, like a, I think that's something that if you look at ambience notion, you should be able to see like campaigns that Sydney created.
So they had like a gun detection campaign and it was basically as part of this campaign, Sydney had like every week what we would cover, like what are the articles that would be part of that week.
And I think it's way more in-depth probably than Airbyte's campaign is going to be, but I think it's like a good example of maybe what we could do here.
And then for SEO, and we're basically trying to imitate these people, right?
We're like trying to mimic their voice.
Okay, how, do they, do they already write?
Not that I've found so far, but we can definitely ask if they have writing examples.
Okay, okay.
Okay.
Yeah, I think that's a good, I think we have like enough information here for at least I think eight articles.
And if they were to publish one every week, that would put them on track with the December, I think, launch, right?
Yeah.
December is the first launch and then they'll have additional launches into next year.
So like the full product launch won't be until probably like Q2 or Q3.
Okay.
All right.
That makes sense.
And then for the SEO strategy, let me go back.
Okay.
They didn't share any, like, documentation on the product or anything else?
No, because it's still being developed.
Great.
Okay.
Okay, I think I have – there are some things that they were – let me actually just take a look real quick.
Yeah, I think I can use the audience personas and the company context to – Does the company context have their competitors in there?
Or do they see as competitors?
It's a lot.
It's probably not all of them.
Like, no Red Panda was mentioned on the call.
Yeah.
I would go with the ones I would – I would go with the ones that they mentioned.
Okay.
Okay.
So I think – okay, can – like, there is – I think there's definitely –
There's here, but, you know, this is pretty, like, this is, again, technical.
So it's going to be, we're going to have to do, like, another balancing act between, like, what the workflow can do and what we can suggest.
Okay, so let me actually just see what were the competitors that they brought up.
So, Supabase, RedPanda, Merge, Nango.
Yeah, RedPanda, Composio, Nango, Merge, and then Matorial or something like that.
Supabase, they mentioned that in the call, but I don't know if it's so much as a competitor as their old product.
Mm-hmm.
Or existing product.
Okay, what was the competitor, Natorial?
Uh, Matorial.
Bye.
Bye.
Bye.
That one at the bottom.
Unmatorial, okay.
Resources.
Yeah, I don't understand half of what these companies do, so.
Yeah, we're in the same boat.
I understand data, like, what they're doing, like, with data infrastructure, I do understand what it is on, like, a high level.
But, like, as soon as it starts getting into the weeds, I'm lost.
Okay, all right, I think I can get started, like, I'll try to start doing some stuff today for the strategy, like, for the SEO clusters, at least.
And I don't know, when is the, you said the weekly sync was on Friday, but I guess that's where the regular team, like, the, like, the execution team.
Yeah.
Yeah.
Yeah, so I'm seeing what Kyle thinks, if we should schedule a separate session or not.
Okay.
All right, let me just, I think Jerry's on that account, right?
Honestly, I would prefer if it was on Friday, like before this call.
I don't have really like tomorrow and Thursday.
It's all just going to be all meetings, so, but we'll see what they say.
Yeah.
Okay.
I will try to come up with like a 12-week content calendar from all of this.
Yeah, yeah, sure.
Yeah, let me know if have any questions, happy to give it another look.
But like I said, just need to make sure that we have enough like ideas for each blog so that we can actually write.
I think maybe even if you could like prompt the AI to provide like a rough, like not all that.
An outline, but just like a breakdown of like, what's the thesis?
What are the, like, what are our arguments?
Like, I think that would be helpful to determine like, okay, is there enough information in here that we could like talk about?
Yeah.
And if not, then it's just like, okay, we can get started with two and then we'll need to have more interviews.
Yeah, yeah, totally.
Yeah.
Okay.
Sounds like a plan.
All right.
Well, yeah, thanks for meeting up.
I'll keep you posted on the SEO firm.
Okay.
Sounds good.
Bye-bye.
Bye.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Review Airbyte's content strategy and align on next steps.](https://fathom.video/share/NgyUvgR1aZywtBsL9pwHKQsJzadkEY51?tab=summary&timestamp=0.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [**Strategy Pivot:** Replace the pillar-based thought leadership plan with a 12-week publishing schedule.](https://fathom.video/share/NgyUvgR1aZywtBsL9pwHKQsJzadkEY51?tab=summary&timestamp=339.0)
      - [**Rationale:** The current content is based on a single interview, which provides insufficient depth for full articles within a pillar structure.](https://fathom.video/share/NgyUvgR1aZywtBsL9pwHKQsJzadkEY51?tab=summary&timestamp=153.0)
  - [**Content Focus:** Prioritize AI-optimized content (AEO) over traditional SEO, per the client's request.](https://fathom.video/share/NgyUvgR1aZywtBsL9pwHKQsJzadkEY51?tab=summary&timestamp=75.0)
      - [**Note:** AEO is a client-specific term; the underlying mechanism is still SEO, as LLMs source data from Google.](https://fathom.video/share/NgyUvgR1aZywtBsL9pwHKQsJzadkEY51?tab=summary&timestamp=75.0)
  - [**SEO Strategy:** Aida will begin competitor analysis using the client's provided list (RedPanda, Composio, Nango, Merge, Matorial).](https://fathom.video/share/NgyUvgR1aZywtBsL9pwHKQsJzadkEY51?tab=summary&timestamp=496.0)
  - [**Timeline:** The content schedule must support the first product launch in December, with the full launch planned for Q2/Q3 next year.](https://fathom.video/share/NgyUvgR1aZywtBsL9pwHKQsJzadkEY51?tab=summary&timestamp=435.0)

## Topics

### Content Strategy Pivot

  - [**Problem:** The initial pillar-based plan is unworkable.](https://fathom.video/share/NgyUvgR1aZywtBsL9pwHKQsJzadkEY51?tab=summary&timestamp=153.0)
      - [**Rationale:** It relies on a single interview, which lacks the depth for multiple, distinct articles per pillar.](https://fathom.video/share/NgyUvgR1aZywtBsL9pwHKQsJzadkEY51?tab=summary&timestamp=153.0)
      - [**Risk:** This could lead to shallow, repetitive content, a known issue from the Hypergrowth project.](https://fathom.video/share/NgyUvgR1aZywtBsL9pwHKQsJzadkEY51?tab=summary&timestamp=263.0)
  - [**Solution:** A 12-week publishing schedule.](https://fathom.video/share/NgyUvgR1aZywtBsL9pwHKQsJzadkEY51?tab=summary&timestamp=180.0)
      - [**Format:** One \~700–800 word Substack article per week.](https://fathom.video/share/NgyUvgR1aZywtBsL9pwHKQsJzadkEY51?tab=summary&timestamp=240.0)
      - [**Validation:** Each topic must have a clear thesis and supporting arguments to ensure sufficient depth.](https://fathom.video/share/NgyUvgR1aZywtBsL9pwHKQsJzadkEY51?tab=summary&timestamp=706.0)
      - [**Example:** The Ambiance "gun detection" campaign is a model for this structured approach.](https://fathom.video/share/NgyUvgR1aZywtBsL9pwHKQsJzadkEY51?tab=summary&timestamp=358.0)

### SEO Strategy & Competitor Analysis

  - [**Constraint:** No product documentation is available, as the product is still in development.](https://fathom.video/share/NgyUvgR1aZywtBsL9pwHKQsJzadkEY51?tab=summary&timestamp=468.0)
  - [**Competitors for Analysis:**](https://fathom.video/share/NgyUvgR1aZywtBsL9pwHKQsJzadkEY51?tab=summary&timestamp=504.0)
      - [RedPanda](https://fathom.video/share/NgyUvgR1aZywtBsL9pwHKQsJzadkEY51?tab=summary&timestamp=561.0)
      - [Composio](https://fathom.video/share/NgyUvgR1aZywtBsL9pwHKQsJzadkEY51?tab=summary&timestamp=561.0)
      - [Nango](https://fathom.video/share/NgyUvgR1aZywtBsL9pwHKQsJzadkEY51?tab=summary&timestamp=561.0)
      - [Merge](https://fathom.video/share/NgyUvgR1aZywtBsL9pwHKQsJzadkEY51?tab=summary&timestamp=561.0)
      - [Matorial](https://fathom.video/share/NgyUvgR1aZywtBsL9pwHKQsJzadkEY51?tab=summary&timestamp=561.0)
      - [Supabase (not a direct competitor; it's Airbyte's existing product)](https://fathom.video/share/NgyUvgR1aZywtBsL9pwHKQsJzadkEY51?tab=summary&timestamp=561.0)

## Next Steps

  - [**Erik:**](https://fathom.video/share/NgyUvgR1aZywtBsL9pwHKQsJzadkEY51?tab=summary&timestamp=693.0)
      - [Draft a 12-week content calendar with thesis/argument breakdowns for each topic.](https://fathom.video/share/NgyUvgR1aZywtBsL9pwHKQsJzadkEY51?tab=summary&timestamp=694.0)
      - [Confirm the new strategy with Kyle.](https://fathom.video/share/NgyUvgR1aZywtBsL9pwHKQsJzadkEY51?tab=summary&timestamp=299.0)
      - [Request writing samples from the client to inform brand voice.](https://fathom.video/share/NgyUvgR1aZywtBsL9pwHKQsJzadkEY51?tab=summary&timestamp=400.0)
  - [**Aida:**](https://fathom.video/share/NgyUvgR1aZywtBsL9pwHKQsJzadkEY51?tab=summary&timestamp=496.0)
      - [Begin SEO competitor analysis using the provided list.](https://fathom.video/share/NgyUvgR1aZywtBsL9pwHKQsJzadkEY51?tab=summary&timestamp=496.0)
  - [**Erik & Aida:**](https://fathom.video/share/NgyUvgR1aZywtBsL9pwHKQsJzadkEY51?tab=summary&timestamp=660.0)
      - [Schedule a separate strategy sync, ideally before the Friday execution team call.](https://fathom.video/share/NgyUvgR1aZywtBsL9pwHKQsJzadkEY51?tab=summary&timestamp=660.0)

action_items.description: Email client: request open-source value prop + writing samples
Draft 8–12 Substack articles (700–800w) w/ thesis + bullets; then build 12-wk calendar
Email Kyle re: separate strategy session; if yes, schedule before Fri weekly sync
